expectations.text: States (statement -> tail-recursion) #9 is NOT available for this tree recursion (no single tail call)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Rests solely on TCO: "Most languages (JS included) don't do tail-call elimination, so recursion depth == nesting depth == live stack frames. No tweak to the recursive call saves an input..." It never explains tree/branching recursion has more than one recursive call / no single tail position; no no-tail-form structural point.

expectations.text: Recommends an explicit-stack (if -> while) iteration or a generator-as-state-machine
